h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought the exact line would do the caption’s work because the words are identical both times.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, I get it: address first, verb second, because the copied words alone cannot prove "turned back."
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was proceeding on the assumption that identical wording inherently guarantees identical semantic function across different speakers.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Keep the test narrow. The badge image can guide the caption, but the excerpt has to carry the claim.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts from evaluating the verb itself to establishing a strict evidentiary standard based on the text's physical actions to manage the learner's premature attachment to a flashy verb.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: “Turned back” is worth testing, not dropping. Put a pencil under three bits of evidence in the second excerpt: who has the floor, who the line is aimed at now, and what the room does after it.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor adapts to the learner's proposed verb by transforming it from a premature conclusion into a testable hypothesis with specific textual criteria.
